spec_match.rationale: Builds an Attribution-Reporting-Register-Trigger payload with arbitrary length trigger source_keys exactly as the v131 change permits, while preserving generated 128-bit hex key_piece entries and aggregatable_values mappings.

interactive.rationale: Add key, add a 200-character key, add duplicate key, set over budget, reset, and per-row key/value inputs all drive the live JSON and validation readouts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature card now promises live duplicate, empty-key, key-piece, and contribution-budget validation alongside arbitrary-length descriptive trigger keys.

edge_cases.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP reproduced the previous always-valid duplicate-key collapse, then verified the repair: duplicate keys, empty keys, empty/non-uint32 values, generated key_piece shape, and L1 contribution-budget overflow now surface as invalid states while 200-character trigger keys remain valid.

design_system.rationale: The dynamic rows now use CSS classes and design-system variables for borders, backgrounds, text, validation states, and responsive layout; the raw inline #000/#fff styles were removed.

summary: Payload builder now keeps long trigger key identifiers valid while surfacing the remaining validation boundaries: duplicate and empty source_keys, generated key_piece shape, non-negative 32-bit values, and L1 contribution-budget overflow. Dynamic form styling now uses design-system variables instead of raw inline hex.
